mcqs.question: What is abstraction in OOP?
mcqs.explanation: Abstraction is the process of exposing only the necessary information to the outside world while hiding the implementation details.

mcqs.question: What is encapsulation in OOP?
mcqs.explanation: Encapsulation is the concept of bundling data and its associated methods that operate on that data within a single unit.

mcqs.question: What is the purpose of access specifiers in C++?

options.text: To control access to class members

mcqs.explanation: Access specifiers are used to control access to class members and achieve encapsulation.

mcqs.question: What is the difference between public and private access specifiers?

options.text: Public allows access from anywhere, while private restricts access
Public restricts access, while private allows access from anywhere
Public and private are the same
Public is used for data members, while private is used for member functions

mcqs.explanation: Public access specifier allows access to class members from anywhere, while private access specifier restricts access to class members.

mcqs.question: What is the purpose of abstract classes in C++?

options.text: To provide a common base class for derived classes

mcqs.explanation: Abstract classes are used to achieve abstraction and provide a common base class for derived classes.

mcqs.question: What is the difference between abstraction and encapsulation?

options.text: Abstraction focuses on hiding implementation details, while encapsulation focuses on bundling data and methods
Abstraction and encapsulation are the same
Abstraction focuses on bundling data and methods, while encapsulation focuses on hiding implementation details
Abstraction is used for data members, while encapsulation is used for member functions

mcqs.explanation: Abstraction focuses on hiding implementation details, while encapsulation focuses on bundling data and methods.

mcqs.question: What is data hiding in OOP?

options.text: Hiding the internal details of an object from the outside world

mcqs.explanation: Data hiding is the concept of hiding the internal details of an object from the outside world.

mcqs.question: What is the purpose of access specifiers in C++?

options.text: To control access to class members

mcqs.explanation: Access specifiers are used to control access to class members and achieve encapsulation.

mcqs.question: What is the difference between protected and private access specifiers?

options.text: Protected allows access within the class and its derived classes, while private restricts access
Protected restricts access, while private allows access within the class and its derived classes
Protected and private are the same
Protected is used for data members, while private is used for member functions

mcqs.explanation: Protected access specifier allows access to class members within the class and its derived classes, while private access specifier restricts access to class members.
